algorithm: Direct baseline EOH publishing directly to /ackermann_cmd
website_caption: This bag captured an early real-hardware EOH run using direct LiDAR-to-Ackermann control. It demonstrates the baseline reactive wall-balancing controller before later tuning.

algorithm: Final or near-final EOH with steering-based speed cap
website_caption: This bag represents a successful real-track run using the tuned EOH controller. It provides strong evidence that the final reactive controller could complete the physical F1TENTH course.
This run tested more aggressive race-speed settings and exposed cases where the car entered turns too quickly.

algorithm: Aggressive EOH race-speed tuning with adjusted gates
website_caption: This bag captured a later race-track tuning attempt after speed gates were adjusted.

algorithm: EOH tuning with modified steering sensitivity
website_caption: This bag tested steering sensitivity changes and helped reveal when the controller became too aggressive or unstable.

algorithm: Stable EOH retest with crawl/stop threshold changes
website_caption: This run returned to a more stable EOH baseline and tested softer crawl behavior instead of hard stopping.

algorithm: Improved EOH with earlier turn reaction
website_caption: This bag captured an improved EOH configuration that completed most of the track but still struggled with a tight turn.
This bag provides a manually driven scan reference of the physical track.
